text:
I'll search for this reference to verify whether it exists.
Based on my web search, I found the actual paper being referenced.

The cited reference uses the title "factual and graphical approaches to causality," but my search revealed that the actual paper is titled 
"Single World Intervention Graphs (SWIGs): A Unification of the Counterfactual and Graphical Approaches to Causality"
. 

The authors are Thomas S. Richardson and James M. Robins, and 
it was published in April 2013 as CSSS Working Paper #128 from the Center for Statistics and the Social Sciences
. The venue and year match the citation provided, and 
it is identified as Working Paper Number 128, Center for Statistics and the Social Sciences, University of Washington
.

The cited title "factual and graphical approaches to causality" is an inaccurate truncation or paraphrase of the actual title, which contains the key phrase "counterfactual" rather than "factual," and includes "Single World Intervention Graphs (SWIGs)" and "Unification" as crucial title elements. The authors are not listed in the citation metadata provided, but they are Richardson and Robins.

EXPLANATION: The cited title is an incomplete or inaccurate paraphrase of the real paper by Richardson and Robins; the actual title is "Single World Intervention Graphs (SWIGs): A Unification of the Counterfactual and Graphical Approaches to Causality." The venue, year, and working paper series all match, but the title is significantly truncated and uses "factual" instead of "counterfactual."
LINK: https://csss.uw.edu/research/working-papers/single-world-intervention-graphs-swigs-unification-counterfactual-and
FOUND_TITLE: Single World Intervention Graphs (SWIGs): A Unification of the Counterfactual and Graphical Approaches to Causality
FOUND_AUTHORS: Thomas S. Richardson, James M. Robins
FOUND_YEAR: 2013
VERDICT: UNLIKELY
